mechanism_narrative: Persistently low and stable federal funds rate near cycle lows signals accommodative financial conditions that lower the equity discount rate and support risk asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.79% in March 2017 vintage, near multi-year lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20-day SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return hit rate during low-rate periods does not exceed unconditional base rate of 65.5% by a statistically significant margin (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede broader economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% (Mar 2017 vintage), near cycle lows, trigger not currently active
failure_condition: In triggered episodes historically, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a simultaneously low or falling federal funds rate indicates robust consumer demand unconstrained by tightening credit conditions, jointly producing above-average S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither series alone is sufficient without the other's confirming signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND FEDFUNDS below its 12-month moving average
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform single-factor RSAFS-only signal by a Rank IC margin of at least 0.02, or joint-trigger 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue momentum, which is quickly priced into equities and produces modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 103.7332 in Feb 2017 vintage showing continued expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO change and forward 5-day SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sustained payroll employment growth above trend signals labor market tightness that raises wage-cost pressure and squeezes future corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter even though near-term growth appears strong.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 6-month average monthly gain > 200k while UNRATE below 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS 145858k (Mar 2017 vintage), UNRATE 4.5%, consistent with tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightness
failure_condition: During periods meeting the trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, contradicting the margin-compression thesis, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 8% signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and often precedes broader consumer retrenchment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as the market anticipates slower growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from vintage 1288.0 to revised 1271.0 (Feb 2017), a moderate decline below trigger threshold
regime_hint: any regime, most powerful when FEDFUNDS is rising
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show 20-day forward SPX returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or the mean triggered return is not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected acceleration in CPI inflation combined with a federal funds rate still near historic lows creates a policy-lag mismatch where markets anticipate faster future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as risk premia adjust; the effect requires both the inflation surprise and the low-rate starting point.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized change > 3% AND FEDFUNDS < 1.5%
regime_hint: early monetary tightening cycle with low starting rates
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to produce negative mean 5-day forward returns significantly different from zero (p > 0.05), or hit rate of negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of steady real GDP growth without acceleration or deceleration, the S&P 500 tends to drift upward over a quarterly horizon as stable macro conditions support consistent earnings growth expectations and low realized volatility risk premia.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value 16813.3 (Oct 2016) reflecting continued moderate growth trend
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing GDPC1 growth rate and forward 60-day SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive-return hit rate does not exceed the unconditional 65.5% base rate significantly.
